title: Modern Defense: Pterodactyl
summary: Learn the Modern Defense with the Pterodactyl, where Black creates an unusual pawn structure for dynamic play.
description: The Modern Defense is a flexible opening where Black allows White to build then attacks. The Pterodactyl is a tricky system where Black fianchettoes and plays ...b5 early.
line: 1.e4 g6 2.d4 Bg7 3.Nc3 d6 4.Nf3 a6 5.a4 b6 6.Bg5 Bg4
objectives: Understand the Modern idea: let White build, then strike back.
Learn the Pterodactyl: unusual but dangerous.
Master flexible play in asymmetric positions.
plans: White uses extra space to attack, but must be careful of Black's counterplay.
Black seeks piece activity and pawn breaks.
The Pterodactyl is tricky: both sides have unusual plans.
commonMistakes: Do not underestimate Black's setup. It's solid.
Avoid passive play. Keep the initiative.
Do not ignore Black's queenside play.
completionTitle: Dynamic position with chances for both
completionSummary: The Modern Defense teaches flexible play and dynamic counterattack.

steps.title: Control the center
steps.instruction: Play e4 to control central space.
steps.coaching: White controls the center. Black will respond with the Modern.
steps.hint: Push the king pawn two squares.

steps.title: The Modern Defense
steps.coaching: This is the Modern: Black avoids early theory and develops flexibly.

steps.title: Claim central space
steps.instruction: Play d4 to establish central control.
steps.coaching: White builds the center. Black will attack it with pieces.
steps.hint: Push the queen pawn to d4.

steps.title: Black fianchettoes
steps.coaching: This is the Modern setup: bishop on g7 gives strong diagonal pressure.

steps.title: Develop the knight
steps.instruction: Move Nc3, developing toward the center.
steps.coaching: Natural development. White is building a central position.
steps.hint: Develop the queen's knight to c3.

steps.title: Black solidifies
steps.coaching: Black prepares development while keeping the position solid.

steps.title: Develop the knight
steps.coaching: White develops naturally, preparing for castling.

steps.title: The Pterodactyl begins
steps.instruction: Play a6, preparing the b5 break.
steps.coaching: This is the Pterodactyl: Black plays ...a6, then ...b5, creating queenside pressure.

steps.title: Prevent b5
steps.instruction: Play a4 to prevent the b5 break.
steps.coaching: White prevents the break. But Black has other ideas.

steps.title: Black prepares b5 anyway
steps.instruction: Play b6, keeping the b5 idea alive.
steps.coaching: Black prepares b5 anyway. The battle continues on the queenside.

steps.title: Develop the bishop
steps.coaching: White develops actively. The center is heating up.

steps.title: Black develops
steps.coaching: Black develops. The Pterodactyl is taking shape.
